explanation:
Unit conversions are the foundation of science, engineering, and everyday life. The metric system (SI) is the global standard. The US and some territories maintain the imperial system.

## How It's Calculated

To convert BTU to kilocalories for air conditioning, use the standard international formula. 1 BTU ≈ 0.252 kilocalories. This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside reasonable parameters.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: How do I convert BTU to kilocalories?
faq.a: Enter the BTU value, select your conversion direction (BTU to kcal or kcal to BTU), and the calculator will instantly show the converted result using the standard international formula.

faq.q: What's the conversion formula from BTU to kcal?
faq.a: The standard formula is: 1 BTU = 0.252 kilocalories. Simply multiply your BTU value by 0.252 to get kilocalories.

faq.q: What size AC unit do I need in BTU?
faq.a: For typical rooms: 100–150 sq ft requires 5,000–8,000 BTU; 250–300 sq ft needs 10,000–12,000 BTU; 450+ sq ft requires 18,000+ BTU. Use our calculator to convert to kcal if needed.
Results are within ±5% accuracy using standard international formulas. For critical HVAC decisions, consult an HVAC professional.

faq.q: How often is the data updated?
faq.a: The conversion formula is constant and doesn't require external updates. We verify our data quarterly to ensure accuracy.
Yes, like all Hacé Cuentas calculators, this tool is 100% free with no registration required.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send your data to any server.

faq.q: What's the difference between BTU and kilocalories?
faq.a: Both measure heat energy. 1 BTU = 0.252 kcal. BTU is used mainly in North America and UK; kilocalories are metric-based and used globally.

faq.q: Can I use this for commercial AC systems?
faq.a: Yes, the conversion formula works for any size. However, commercial AC sizing involves load calculations and other factors—consult an HVAC engineer for precise specifications.
